body:
## Related roadmap items

- Primary: #9 — EH-109
- Secondary: #12 — EH-112
- Provenance/schema contract: #3 — EH-103

## Problem

A correctly extracted routine result such as ALT `21 U/L` with 90% extraction confidence can lack explicit serum/plasma evidence. The UI must not present this metadata gap as an OCR failure or force the user to choose an unstated specimen.

## Updated architecture

EasyHealth is performing a pre-launch Registry 2.0 hard cutover. The expected state is registry-native `partial`, not legacy `compatibility_only`:

- ALT and catalytic activity in `U/L` are recognized;
- serum and plasma remain separate concrete definitions;
- missing specimen prevents concrete resolution;
- raw acceptance remains available;
- technical candidate details are collapsed by default;
- no Registry v1 runtime fallback is required.

## User-facing copy

> ALT recognized
>
> The specimen is not stated in this report. You can still accept this result; precise measurement mapping will remain pending.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] ALT `21 U/L` without specimen returns `partial`, not `unmapped` or `resolved`.
- [ ] Extraction confidence remains separate from mapping state.
- [ ] Accepting the raw result does not require a serum/plasma choice.
- [ ] The system does not infer a specimen absent from source evidence.
- [ ] Technical resolver details and manual mapping are collapsed by default.
- [ ] Automated checks cover partial ALT, explicit serum/plasma resolution and incompatible units.
title: ALT without specimen must be partial, not unmapped

**Sprint:** Sprint 5 (2026-09-07 – 2026-09-18)
**Epic:** Readiness
**Priority:** P0 · **Story points:** 5 · **Risk:** High
**Functional owner:** Backend / QA
**Dependencies:** EH-141,EH-142

## Goal
Return no score when minimum groups are not satisfied and expose machine-readable readiness reasons.

## Checklist
- [ ] Readiness evaluator
- [ ] group alternatives
- [ ] null score API
- [ ] missing/invalid/outdated reason codes
- [ ] tests

## Acceptance criteria
All eight systems return null when required groups fail; no partial average is presented as a valid system score.

## Sprint goal
Finalize transparent current-state assessments for eight body systems.

**Due date:** 2026-09-18
title: [EH-143] Implement readiness and null-result contract

body:
**Roadmap ID:** `EH-142`
**Sprint:** Sprint 5 (2026-09-07 – 2026-09-18)
**Epic:** Assessment engine
**Priority:** P0 · **Story points:** 5 · **Risk:** High
**Functional owner:** Backend / QA
**Dependencies:** EH-121, EH-141

## Goal
Limit assessment to verified numeric observations with a reviewed concrete measurement definition, approved assessment binding, and usable document-native reference range.

## Checklist
- [ ] Resolved-definition and reviewed-binding eligibility predicate
- [ ] Verification requirement
- [ ] Partial, ambiguous and unmapped exclusion
- [ ] Qualitative-value exclusion
- [ ] Missing-range handling
- [ ] Exact exclusion reasons and tests

## Acceptance criteria
Partial, ambiguous, unmapped, provisional, unverified, qualitative or range-ineligible observations never enter scoring. The UI can state the exact exclusion reason without implying that the source laboratory result is invalid.

## Sprint goal
Finalize transparent assessment eligibility over Registry 2.0 observations.

**Due date:** 2026-09-18
title: [EH-142] Enforce resolved, verified range and value eligibility

body:
﻿**Roadmap ID:** `EH-133`
**Sprint:** Sprint 6 (2026-09-21 – 2026-10-02)
**Epic:** Knowledge architecture
**Priority:** P0 · **Story points:** 5 · **Risk:** Medium
**Functional owner:** Clinical Product / Frontend
**Dependencies:** EH-102,EH-125

## Goal
Create a content layer for biomarker and panel education that is independent from observation normalization and assessment.

## Checklist
- [ ] Article types
- [ ] slug
- [ ] locale
- [ ] contentVersion
- [ ] review status
- [ ] reviewedBy/At
- [ ] source list
- [ ] related measurement keys
- [ ] deprecation policy

## Acceptance criteria
Content can be updated without changing score logic; every published article has version, sources and review metadata.

## Sprint goal
Launch a safe educational layer that is separated from the assessment engine.

**Due date:** 2026-10-02
title: [EH-133] Define version-controlled Knowledge Base content schema

Create consistent deep links between a measurement, its historical series, source document and system assessment.

body:
﻿**Roadmap ID:** `EH-130`
**Sprint:** Sprint 4 (2026-08-24 – 2026-09-04)
**Epic:** Data quality
**Priority:** P1 · **Story points:** 5 · **Risk:** Medium
**Functional owner:** Backend / Frontend
**Dependencies:** None

## Goal
Identify identical or near-identical uploads without deleting potentially distinct medical events.

## Checklist
- [ ] File hash
- [ ] metadata similarity
- [ ] duplicate candidate state
- [ ] user choice
- [ ] safe archive
- [ ] audit event

## Acceptance criteria
Exact duplicates are detected; no document is silently deleted; user can retain both when events differ.

## Sprint goal
Connect documents, measurements and panels into a chronological health timeline.

body:

## Goal
Compare the same measurement definition over time across laboratories and unit variants.

## Checklist
- [ ] Normalized series
- [ ] document-native ranges per point
- [ ] unit conversion guard
- [ ] date selector
- [ ] source navigation

## Acceptance criteria
Only compatible measurement definitions are combined; every point retains its original unit/range/source; incompatible series are separated.

## Sprint goal
Connect documents, measurements and panels into a chronological health timeline.

Group normalized observations using panel definitions while preserving ungrouped measurements.

body:
﻿**Roadmap ID:** `EH-124`
**Sprint:** Sprint 3 (2026-08-10 – 2026-08-21)
**Epic:** Quality
**Priority:** P0 · **Story points:** 3 · **Risk:** Medium
**Functional owner:** QA / Frontend
**Dependencies:** EH-118,EH-120,EH-121

## Goal
Validate the review flow against representative documents, keyboard usage and failure cases.

## Checklist
- [ ] PDF/image matrix
- [ ] keyboard navigation
- [ ] screen-reader labels
- [ ] long values
- [ ] missing ranges
- [ ] offline/retry
- [ ] regression report

## Acceptance criteria
P0 paths pass; no inaccessible blocking controls; defects are triaged before release gate.

## Sprint goal
Give users a reliable review workflow that links each normalized observation to the source document and supports corrections.

## Release gate
Verified data loop accepted

**Due date:** 2026-08-21

body:
﻿**Roadmap ID:** `EH-123`
**Sprint:** Sprint 3 (2026-08-10 – 2026-08-21)
**Epic:** Assessment integrity
**Priority:** P0 · **Story points:** 3 · **Risk:** High
**Functional owner:** Backend
**Dependencies:** EH-120,EH-121,EH-122

## Goal
Ensure corrections, verification and reprocessing propagate to charts and Health Profile deterministically.

## Checklist
- [ ] Dependency event
- [ ] cache invalidation
- [ ] recalculation job
- [ ] version stamping
- [ ] failure recovery

## Acceptance criteria
Changed observations update derived outputs exactly once; failed recalculation is visible and retryable; old score version remains auditable.

## Sprint goal
Give users a reliable review workflow that links each normalized observation to the source document and supports corrections.

**Due date:** 2026-08-21
title: [EH-123] Invalidate and recalculate dependent assessments after changes

body:
﻿**Roadmap ID:** `EH-122`
**Sprint:** Sprint 3 (2026-08-10 – 2026-08-21)
**Epic:** Review UX
**Priority:** P1 · **Story points:** 5 · **Risk:** Medium
**Functional owner:** Frontend / Backend
**Dependencies:** EH-121

## Goal
Let users verify multiple high-confidence observations while keeping ambiguous or edited rows individual.

## Checklist
- [ ] Selection rules
- [ ] bulk verify action
- [ ] exclude ambiguous/unmapped
- [ ] confirmation summary
- [ ] undo via audit-safe transition

## Acceptance criteria
Only eligible rows can be batch verified; the UI states how many were skipped and why.

## Sprint goal
Give users a reliable review workflow that links each normalized observation to the source document and supports corrections.

**Due date:** 2026-08-21
title: [EH-122] Add batch verification for low-risk exact matches

body:
﻿**Roadmap ID:** `EH-121`
**Sprint:** Sprint 3 (2026-08-10 – 2026-08-21)
**Epic:** Auditability
**Priority:** P0 · **Story points:** 3 · **Risk:** Medium
**Functional owner:** Backend
**Dependencies:** EH-119, EH-120

## Goal
Keep an immutable history of corrections, verification changes and reprocessing results.

## Checklist
- [ ] Audit event schema
- [ ] before/after diff
- [ ] actor/version metadata
- [ ] history endpoint
- [ ] compact UI

## Acceptance criteria
Users/support can see what changed and why; audit rows are append-only; sensitive raw document text is not duplicated.

## Sprint goal
Give users a reliable review workflow that links each normalized observation to the source document and supports corrections.

**Due date:** 2026-08-21
title: [EH-121] Add observation change history

body:
**Roadmap ID:** `EH-120`
**Sprint:** Sprint 3 (2026-08-10 – 2026-08-21)
**Epic:** Verification workflow
**Priority:** P0 · **Story points:** 3 · **Risk:** Medium
**Functional owner:** Backend / Frontend
**Dependencies:** EH-104, EH-112

## Goal
Define the user workflow from automatic mapping or incomplete recognition to verified, corrected, retained-partial, rejected or superseded outcomes.

## Checklist
- [ ] State machine across resolver and verification dimensions
- [ ] UI actions and permissions
- [ ] Actor/timestamp/reason
- [ ] Batch API contract
- [ ] Partial/ambiguous verification restrictions
- [ ] Reprocessing/manual-decision transitions

## Acceptance criteria
Only allowed transitions occur. Verification records actor/time. Partial or ambiguous rows cannot be marked as a verified concrete measurement without a reviewed definition, but their raw result may remain accepted and visible.

## Sprint goal
Make verification durable across every Registry 2.0 resolver state.

**Due date:** 2026-08-21
title: [EH-120] Implement verification transitions for all resolver states

body:
**Roadmap ID:** `EH-119`
**Sprint:** Sprint 3 (2026-08-10 – 2026-08-21)
**Epic:** Corrections
**Priority:** P0 · **Story points:** 5 · **Risk:** High
**Functional owner:** Frontend / Backend
**Dependencies:** EH-118, EH-120

## Goal
Allow users to correct parsed values and reviewed semantic mappings while preserving immutable source extraction and supporting raw acceptance when no concrete mapping is justified.

## Checklist
- [ ] Edit parsed value, unit, reference range and date
- [ ] Reviewed measurement picker constrained by hard evidence
- [ ] Partial/unmapped raw acceptance without forced mapping
- [ ] Reason, actor and timestamp
- [ ] Append-only save/undo mutation
- [ ] Validation and actionable errors

## Acceptance criteria
Corrections never overwrite raw fields. Invalid units/ranges are blocked. A missing specimen or method is not guessed, and a user may keep a result partial or unmapped instead of selecting an incompatible definition.

## Sprint goal
Support safe evidence-based corrections in the Registry 2.0 review workflow.

**Due date:** 2026-08-21
title: [EH-119] Implement observation edit and correction flow

body:
﻿**Roadmap ID:** `EH-118`
**Sprint:** Sprint 3 (2026-08-10 – 2026-08-21)
**Epic:** Source provenance
**Priority:** P0 · **Story points:** 5 · **Risk:** High
**Functional owner:** Backend / Frontend
**Dependencies:** EH-103

## Goal
Persist and render page-level provenance, using bounding boxes when available and graceful page-only fallback otherwise.

## Checklist
- [ ] Page index
- [ ] bounding box contract
- [ ] OCR/LLM provenance adapter
- [ ] highlight overlay
- [ ] fallback state

## Acceptance criteria
Every extracted observation links to a source page; supported observations highlight the source region without misalignment.

## Sprint goal
Give users a reliable review workflow that links each normalized observation to the source document and supports corrections.

**Due date:** 2026-08-21
title: [EH-118] Link observations to source page and region

body:
**Roadmap ID:** `EH-117`
**Sprint:** Sprint 3 (2026-08-10 – 2026-08-21)
**Epic:** Review UX
**Priority:** P0 · **Story points:** 5 · **Risk:** Medium
**Functional owner:** Frontend
**Dependencies:** EH-112, EH-113, EH-118

## Goal
Show source documents and resolved, ambiguous, partial or unmapped observations together with clear state and navigation.

## Checklist
- [ ] Document pane and observation list
- [ ] Page/region navigation and selected-row synchronization
- [ ] Resolution and verification status presentation
- [ ] Raw acceptance without forced mapping
- [ ] Progressive technical details
- [ ] Responsive layout and loading/error states

## Acceptance criteria
A user can select any observation, see its source, understand whether mapping is resolved or incomplete, and preserve the result without guessing missing specimen/method data.

## Sprint goal
Provide a reliable Registry 2.0 review workflow linked to source evidence.

**Due date:** 2026-08-21
title: [EH-117] Build split-view launch observation review workspace

body:
**Roadmap ID:** `EH-116`
**Sprint:** Sprint 2 (2026-07-27 – 2026-08-07)
**Epic:** Reprocessing
**Priority:** P0 · **Story points:** 5 · **Risk:** High
**Functional owner:** Backend / QA
**Dependencies:** EH-103, EH-109, EH-112, EH-115

## Goal
Re-run partial, ambiguous, unmapped and resolved observations against a chosen Registry 2.0 release while retaining raw evidence and prior decisions.

## Checklist
- [ ] Dry-run mode and deterministic diff
- [ ] Idempotency and batch limits
- [ ] Append-only candidate revisions
- [ ] Manual-decision protection
- [ ] Assessment/trend invalidation only after approved activation
- [ ] Admin trigger and audit trail

## Acceptance criteria
A staging batch can be dry-run, reviewed and applied. Prior decisions remain auditable; newly recognized rows can improve without changing raw evidence; scores change only after successful reviewed activation.

## Sprint goal
Make launch catalog improvements safely reprocessable without a legacy fallback.

## Release gate
Reprocessing dry-run passed.

**Due date:** 2026-08-07
title: [EH-116] Build safe Registry 2.0 observation reprocessing

body:
**Roadmap ID:** `EH-115`
**Sprint:** Sprint 2 (2026-07-27 – 2026-08-07)
**Epic:** Observability
**Priority:** P1 · **Story points:** 3 · **Risk:** Medium
**Functional owner:** Backend
**Dependencies:** EH-109, EH-112

## Goal
Store a compact privacy-safe explanation for resolved, ambiguous, partial and unmapped decisions.

## Checklist
- [ ] Accepted/rejected evidence list
- [ ] Candidate scores and maturity
- [ ] Winning rule or missing-axis explanation
- [ ] Rejected candidates and hard conflicts
- [ ] Trace schema/resolver/catalog versions
- [ ] Log redaction

## Acceptance criteria
Support can inspect why a row resolved or remained incomplete without re-running the pipeline, while logs contain no full document text or unnecessary personal data.

## Sprint goal
Make launch resolver decisions reproducible and reviewable.

**Due date:** 2026-08-07
title: [EH-115] Persist mapping decision trace for every resolver state

body:
**Roadmap ID:** `EH-114`
**Sprint:** Sprint 2 (2026-07-27 – 2026-08-07)
**Epic:** Resolver cases
**Priority:** P0 · **Story points:** 5 · **Risk:** High
**Functional owner:** Backend / Clinical Product
**Dependencies:** EH-109, EH-111, EH-112

## Goal
Prevent generic glucose labels from collapsing serum, plasma, whole blood, urine, fasting and post-prandial measurements.

## Checklist
- [ ] Serum and plasma
- [ ] Whole blood and urine
- [ ] Fasting and post-prandial timing
- [ ] Unknown specimen/timing partial behavior
- [ ] Reviewed unit conversions
- [ ] Candidate external-code placeholders only when reviewed

## Acceptance criteria
Concrete variants remain distinct. Explicit evidence selects compatible definitions; unknown specimen or timing produces partial/ambiguous evidence and never invents fasting status.

## Sprint goal
Complete safe glucose identities in the launch catalog.

**Due date:** 2026-08-07
title: [EH-114] Cover glucose specimen and timing variants

body:
**Roadmap ID:** `EH-113`
**Sprint:** Sprint 2 (2026-07-27 – 2026-08-07)
**Epic:** Resolver cases
**Priority:** P0 · **Story points:** 8 · **Risk:** High
**Functional owner:** Backend / QA
**Dependencies:** EH-107, EH-109, EH-111, EH-112

## Goal
Implement reviewed rules and exact fixtures for frequent and sample-specific CBC measurements.

## Checklist
- [ ] Five-part differential absolute versus percent
- [ ] Bands versus segmented neutrophils
- [ ] Automated versus manual differential variants
- [ ] RDW-CV/RDW-SD and reticulocyte variants
- [ ] MPV, PDW and plateletcrit
- [ ] Parenthetical abbreviations and exact sample labels
- [ ] Multilingual and OCR-negative fixtures

## Acceptance criteria
Representative exact, multilingual and OCR-corrupted CBC fixtures resolve correctly or remain partial/ambiguous. Missing context never forces the wrong concrete key.

## Sprint goal
Complete the CBC portion of the launch catalog.

**Due date:** 2026-08-07
title: [EH-113] Cover CBC launch-catalog special cases

body:
**Roadmap ID:** `EH-112`
**Sprint:** Sprint 2 (2026-07-27 – 2026-08-07)
**Epic:** Safety states
**Priority:** P0 · **Story points:** 5 · **Risk:** High
**Functional owner:** Backend / Frontend
**Dependencies:** EH-104, EH-109, EH-111

## Goal
Preserve and expose recognized-but-incomplete, conflicting and unknown laboratory results without forcing a mapping or losing raw data.

## Checklist
- [ ] Persist resolved, ambiguous, partial and unmapped
- [ ] Nullable analyte and measurement-definition identity
- [ ] API serialization and evidence
- [ ] Raw acceptance for every state
- [ ] UI wording and technical-details contract
- [ ] Trends/assessment exclusion rules
- [ ] Metrics and fixtures

## Acceptance criteria
Partial, ambiguous and unmapped rows retain raw evidence and remain visible/reprocessable. Only resolved reviewed definitions enter definition-specific trends; only reviewed assessment bindings may affect scoring.

## Sprint goal
Make incomplete semantic identity a safe first-class product state.

**Due date:** 2026-08-07
title: [EH-112] Implement partial, ambiguous and unmapped outcomes end-to-end

body:
**Roadmap ID:** `EH-111`
**Sprint:** Sprint 2 (2026-07-27 – 2026-08-07)
**Epic:** Context-aware resolver
**Priority:** P0 · **Story points:** 5 · **Risk:** High
**Functional owner:** Backend
**Dependencies:** EH-109, EH-110

## Goal
Use value kind, unit family and specimen policy as hard or soft evidence without guessing missing context.

## Checklist
- [ ] Unit-family and value-kind matcher
- [ ] Specimen compatibility
- [ ] Conversion availability after concrete resolution only
- [ ] Invalid combination handling
- [ ] Missing-context partial/ambiguous behavior
- [ ] Sample-unit fixtures and tests

## Acceptance criteria
Percent cannot map to absolute count; urine glucose cannot map to blood glucose; qualitative results do not require numeric units; missing specimen remains partial or ambiguous instead of being inferred.

## Sprint goal
Make hard compatibility safe for the launch catalog.

**Due date:** 2026-08-07
title: [EH-111] Implement unit, value-kind and specimen compatibility rules

body:
**Roadmap ID:** `EH-110`
**Sprint:** Sprint 2 (2026-07-27 – 2026-08-07)
**Epic:** Alias curation
**Priority:** P0 · **Story points:** 5 · **Risk:** Medium
**Functional owner:** Backend / Clinical Product
**Dependencies:** EH-101, EH-102

## Goal
Replace plain aliases with reviewed records that explain language, laboratory/fixture source, match type, confidence authority and lifecycle.

## Checklist
- [ ] AliasDefinition type
- [ ] Exact, normalized, OCR and bounded fuzzy match types
- [ ] Language, laboratory and fixture source
- [ ] Approval/deprecation status
- [ ] One-time Registry v1 alias migration, not a runtime adapter
- [ ] Exact aliases from the 44-row sample corpus

## Acceptance criteria
The resolver explains which alias matched and its provenance. Parenthetical and abbreviated sample labels are recognized. Deprecated, OCR or unreviewed aliases cannot silently receive reviewed exact-match authority.

## Sprint goal
Make launch label coverage measurable and auditable.

**Due date:** 2026-08-07
title: [EH-110] Add alias provenance and launch-fixture matching policy

body:
**Roadmap ID:** `EH-109`
**Sprint:** Sprint 2 (2026-07-27 – 2026-08-07)
**Epic:** Context-aware resolver
**Priority:** P0 · **Story points:** 8 · **Risk:** High
**Functional owner:** Backend / Clinical Product
**Dependencies:** EH-102, EH-107

## Goal
Score reviewed and provisional candidates using normalized label, value kind, unit family, specimen, method, timing, panel context, reference shape and neighbouring rows without forcing incomplete evidence into a concrete identity.

## Checklist
- [ ] Candidate generation across reviewed/provisional maturity
- [ ] Structured accepted/rejected evidence
- [ ] Hard conflicts and deterministic tie-breaking
- [ ] Mapping confidence independent from extraction confidence
- [ ] Partial outcome with missing-axis evidence
- [ ] No LLM-only final mapping
- [ ] Unit and corpus tests

## Acceptance criteria
Identical inputs produce identical candidates, state, confidence band and evidence trace. One reviewed compatible candidate resolves; ties remain ambiguous; recognized but incomplete evidence becomes partial; unknown labels remain unmapped.

## Sprint goal
Resolve raw rows safely within the single launch catalog.

**Due date:** 2026-08-07
title: [EH-109] Implement resolver evidence scoring and partial recognition

body:
**Roadmap ID:** `EH-108`
**Sprint:** Sprint 1 (2026-07-13 – 2026-07-24)
**Epic:** Documentation
**Priority:** P1 · **Story points:** 3 · **Risk:** Medium
**Functional owner:** Tech Lead
**Dependencies:** EH-102, EH-103, EH-105, EH-106

## Goal
Document why EasyHealth uses one pre-launch Registry 2.0 runtime, how Registry v1 knowledge is migrated, how partial identity works, and how to deploy/reset/verify the hard cutover.

## Checklist
- [ ] Architecture decision and rejected dual-runtime alternative
- [ ] Registry/analyte/measurement/observation schema diagram
- [ ] Definition maturity and resolver-state policy
- [ ] Launch catalog and assessment-binding ownership
- [ ] Clean-database migration/reset checklist
- [ ] Launch manifest and corpus release checklist
- [ ] Post-launch registry version/rollback policy

## Acceptance criteria
A new engineer can explain the single-runtime data flow, run the clean cutover, verify that Registry v1 is not imported at runtime, and interpret launch coverage evidence.

## Sprint goal
Make the hard-cutover architecture deployable and auditable.

**Due date:** 2026-07-24
title: [EH-108] Write the Registry 2.0 hard-cutover ADR and launch checklist

body:
**Roadmap ID:** `EH-107`
**Sprint:** Sprint 1 (2026-07-13 – 2026-07-24)
**Epic:** Regression testing
**Priority:** P0 · **Story points:** 5 · **Risk:** Medium
**Functional owner:** QA / Backend
**Dependencies:** EH-102

## Goal
Protect high-risk CBC identity distinctions and the exact printed forms present in the launch sample before the Registry 2.0 cutover.

## Checklist
- [ ] Neutrophils/lymphocytes/monocytes/eosinophils/basophils absolute versus percent
- [ ] RDW-CV versus RDW-SD
- [ ] Reticulocytes absolute versus percent
- [ ] RBC, HGB, HCT, MCV, MCH and MCHC exact-label fixtures
- [ ] Platelets, MPV, PDW and plateletcrit
- [ ] Segmented, band and manual differential variants
- [ ] Compatible and invalid unit cases

## Acceptance criteria
Positive, negative, ambiguous and partial fixtures fail if the wrong concrete measurement is selected. Exact sample forms such as `Hemoglobin (HGB)`, `NEU%` and manual differential labels are recognized safely.

## Sprint goal
Provide CBC launch evidence for the single Registry 2.0 catalog.

## Release gate
CBC regression suite green.

**Due date:** 2026-07-24
title: [EH-107] Add launch CBC measurement regression fixtures

body:
**Roadmap ID:** `EH-106`
**Sprint:** Sprint 1 (2026-07-13 – 2026-07-24)
**Epic:** Registry 2.0 hard cutover
**Priority:** P0 · **Story points:** 8 · **Risk:** High
**Functional owner:** Backend / QA
**Dependencies:** EH-102, EH-103, EH-104, EH-105

## Goal
Move all runtime consumers to Registry 2.0 and remove Registry v1 adapters, fallback paths and feature flags before launch.

## Checklist
- [ ] Migrate extraction, acceptance and correction paths
- [ ] Migrate trends, reports, structured context and biomarker APIs/UI
- [ ] Migrate unit conversion and health assessment to reviewed Registry 2.0 bindings
- [ ] Remove `legacy_adapter`, compatibility-only and legacy fallback
- [ ] Remove Registry v1/v2 feature flags and dual-read telemetry
- [ ] Prohibit runtime imports of the frozen Registry v1 fixture in CI
- [ ] Run clean build, launch corpus and assessment regression

## Acceptance criteria
Registry 2.0 is the only runtime source of laboratory semantic identity. Disabling a feature flag cannot restore Registry v1 because no legacy runtime remains. The frozen v1 snapshot is accessible only to migration/audit tooling.

## Sprint goal
Finish the pre-launch hard cutover while reset and rollback remain inexpensive.

## Release gate
No runtime Registry v1 imports; launch corpus and assessment regression green.

**Due date:** 2026-07-24
title: [EH-106] Remove Registry v1 runtime and complete the hard cutover

body:
**Roadmap ID:** `EH-105`
**Sprint:** Sprint 1 (2026-07-13 – 2026-07-24)
**Epic:** Pre-launch schema cutover
**Priority:** P0 · **Story points:** 5 · **Risk:** High
**Functional owner:** Backend / QA
**Dependencies:** EH-102, EH-103, EH-104

## Goal
Replace required legacy `biomarker_key` observation identity with source identity, resolver status, nullable analyte identity and nullable concrete measurement identity before production.

## Checklist
- [ ] Replace observation identity and uniqueness constraints
- [ ] Enforce source-extraction idempotency introduced by EH-103
- [ ] Use nullable analyte and measurement-definition links introduced by EH-103
- [ ] Use the append-only active revision linkage introduced by EH-103
- [ ] Update APIs and current consumers to the launch schema
- [ ] Document clean-database migration and development reset

## Acceptance criteria
A clean database supports resolved, ambiguous, partial and unmapped observations without synthetic legacy keys. Local development data may be reset; no nonexistent production backfill or legacy-row compatibility contract is required.

## Sprint goal
Install the Registry 2.0 observation schema before valuable production data exists.

## Release gate
Clean-database migration and launch-corpus acceptance paths pass.

**Due date:** 2026-07-24
title: [EH-105] Cut over observations to Registry 2.0 identity

body:
**Roadmap ID:** `EH-104`
**Sprint:** Sprint 1 (2026-07-13 – 2026-07-24)
**Epic:** Observation provenance
**Priority:** P0 · **Story points:** 3 · **Risk:** Medium
**Functional owner:** Backend
**Dependencies:** EH-102, EH-103

## Goal
Model semantic resolver outcome independently from human/system verification so an incomplete mapping is never treated as a trust decision.

## Checklist
- [ ] Define resolver outcomes: resolved, ambiguous, partial, unmapped
- [ ] Define verification states: auto_verified, user_verified, manually_corrected and pending/rejected as needed
- [ ] Define valid transitions and actor/timestamp requirements
- [ ] Expose both dimensions in API DTOs
- [ ] Add database validation and fixtures

## Acceptance criteria
Resolver outcome and verification status are stored separately; invalid transitions are rejected; partial or ambiguous rows cannot become concretely verified without selecting a reviewed definition.

## Sprint goal
Make resolution maturity and verification trust explicit before launch.

**Due date:** 2026-07-24
title: [EH-104] Separate resolver outcomes from verification status

body:
**Roadmap ID:** `EH-103`
**Sprint:** Sprint 1 (2026-07-13 – 2026-07-24)
**Epic:** Observation provenance
**Priority:** P0 · **Story points:** 8 · **Risk:** High
**Functional owner:** Backend
**Dependencies:** EH-102

## Goal
Make every accepted resolved, ambiguous, partial or unmapped observation auditable and reprocessable from immutable raw evidence and the exact processing releases used.

## Checklist
- [ ] Store exact raw label, value, unit, reference text and source location
- [ ] Store extraction, catalog/digest, resolver and normalization versions when applicable
- [ ] Store resolution status with nullable analyte and measurement-definition links
- [ ] Snapshot raw inputs and versions in append-only normalization revisions
- [ ] Use source extraction identity for idempotent acceptance
- [ ] Enforce write-once provenance
- [ ] Add assessment algorithm version to assessment output

## Acceptance criteria
Every new document-derived observation preserves exact source evidence and applicable release identifiers. Partial and unmapped rows remain valid without fabricated semantic keys. Distinct source rows never overwrite each other merely because time or analyte matches.

## Sprint goal
Establish the first-production observation provenance contract for the single Registry 2.0 runtime.

**Due date:** 2026-07-24
title: [EH-103] Add complete raw and version provenance to observations

body:
**Roadmap ID:** `EH-102`
**Sprint:** Sprint 1 (2026-07-13 – 2026-07-24)
**Epic:** Registry 2.0 launch catalog
**Priority:** P0 · **Story points:** 13 · **Risk:** High
**Functional owner:** Backend / Clinical Product
**Dependencies:** EH-101

## Goal
Create one pre-launch Registry 2.0 runtime that separates analytes from concrete measurements, migrates useful Registry v1 knowledge, recognizes the launch corpus safely, and removes the legacy runtime before production.

## Checklist
- [x] Add explicit analyte and concrete measurement identity axes
- [x] Add definition maturity: provisional, reviewed, retired
- [x] Replace compatibility-only with resolved, ambiguous, partial and unmapped
- [x] Migrate all 113 Registry v1 concepts into explicit Registry 2.0 dispositions
- [x] Add the 44 rows from `lab_data/sample_newest.pdf` as exact launch fixtures
- [x] Add sample gaps including ASO, platelet indices, manual differential and specialty serology/allergy tests
- [x] Replace legacy canonical storage coupling with semantic identity and reviewed assessment bindings
- [x] Publish deterministic launch manifest and multidimensional coverage report

## Acceptance criteria
- Registry 2.0 is the only application runtime registry; Registry v1 is migration/audit input only.
- Every Registry v1 concept is reviewed, provisional, merged or retired; no runtime adapter is required.
- All 44 expected sample row types are at least resolved, ambiguous or partial, with exact raw evidence preserved.
- Missing specimen, timing or method never produces a fabricated concrete identity.
- Only reviewed concrete definitions with reviewed assessment bindings can affect scoring.
- `legacy_adapter`, legacy fallback, dual-read and Registry v1/v2 feature flags are removed before launch.

## Related
- Provenance/schema follow-up: #3, #4, #5
- Runtime cutover: #6
- Resolver and fixture work: #7, #9–#16
- ALT partial-resolution evidence: #63

## Sprint goal
Establish a single versioned launch measurement catalog without carrying a second legacy runtime into production.

## Release gate
Architecture approved; launch manifest and coverage report accepted; strict OpenSpec validation passes.

**Due date:** 2026-07-24
title: [EH-102] Build the pre-launch Registry 2.0 launch catalog

body:
﻿**Roadmap ID:** `EH-101`
**Sprint:** Sprint 1 (2026-07-13 – 2026-07-24)
**Epic:** Registry 2.0
**Priority:** P0 · **Story points:** 3 · **Risk:** Low
**Functional owner:** Backend / Clinical Product
**Dependencies:** None

## Goal
Create an explicit baseline of all canonical keys, aliases, unit conversions, specimen rules, score roles, coverage rules and equivalence groups before introducing Registry 2.0.

## Checklist
- [x] Export current definitions
- [x] identify duplicate/ambiguous aliases
- [x] mark breaking-change risks
- [x] tag registry v1.0.0
- [x] create audit report

## Acceptance criteria
A versioned snapshot exists; every current key is accounted for; known ambiguities are logged; no runtime behavior changes.

## Sprint goal
Upgrade the existing static biomarker catalog into versioned measurement definitions without breaking the current assessment engine.

**Due date:** 2026-07-24
title: [EH-101] Audit and freeze the current biomarker registry v1
